title: GitHub Actions Execution Model, Contexts, and State Boundaries · Release Engineering Workshop
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working workflow execution and state trace for github actions execution model, contexts, and state boundaries. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of workflow execution and state trace while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace event, workflow run, job graph, runner allocation, step process, action execution, and post-step cleanup for one commit.
Distinguish server-side contexts and expression evaluation from runner-side environment expansion, shell parsing, and process state.
Classify workspace files, step outputs, job outputs, environment files, artifacts, caches, summaries, logs, and external releases by scope and lifetime.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Workflow Run Job Step Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop run attempt 2 loses a build result because the team confuses server expressions, runner environments, step processes, job outputs, artifacts, and caches. Trace identity, lifetime, authority, and cleanup.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace event, workflow run, job graph, runner allocation, step process, action execution, and post-step cleanup for one commit. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Run Identity Attempt Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-run-identity-attempt-evidence-eipofh". Tailor its execution-trace.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Run Identity Attempt Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Run Identity Attempt Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Run Identity Attempt Evidence, keep Ci Run Identity Attempt Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-run-identity-attempt-evidence-eipofh
  - competency: ci-run-identity-attempt-evidence
    artifact: execution-trace.yaml
    decision: run identity attempt evidence
    environment: Node 24 TypeScript monorepo with three lockfiles
    fault-injection: make the service container start before it is ready
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: eipofh
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-ref: owner/repo/.github/workflows/ci.yml@refs/pull/42/merge
      run-id: 8842
      run-attempt: 2
      job-runner: fresh-ubuntu-24.04
      step-process: isolated-shell
      state-handoff: validated-job-output
      retained-artifact: test-report-digest
    verify: compare event payload workflow ref workflow sha run id attempt runner and artifact metadata
    failure: Reject shared-runner assumptions, branch-only identity, or cache and artifact state treated as equivalent.
    repair: Trace server and runner evaluation, name each state lifetime, and retain exact run and artifact identity.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use repository, workflow ref, workflow SHA, run ID, run attempt, actor, triggering actor, event, ref, and SHA to identify exact execution evidence.
